# UC-17.1.82 · Splunk SOAR + ISE Closed-Loop Playbook Audit and Mean-Time-to-Containment KPI

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Splunk SOAR is the automation that runs playbooks. When SOAR tells ISE to quarantine a device, we measure how long it takes — and prove the response actually happened.*

---

## Description

Audits Splunk SOAR playbook → ISE ANC closed-loop performance. Measures (a) SOAR `block_endpoint` action success rate, (b) average lag from SOAR action to ISE 86001 confirmation, and (c) max lag (worst case).

## Value

Automated containment is only as fast as its slowest link. SOAR playbook audit gives the SOC measurable evidence that playbooks executed end-to-end, satisfying NIS2/DORA incident-response requirements with quantitative MTTC.

## Implementation

Schedule every 1h.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Run a SOAR `block_endpoint` playbook. Confirm ISE 86001 ANC apply within 30s.

### Negative scenario

All playbooks succeed within target MTTC. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=phantom action_name=block_endpoint
| eval soar_apply_time=_time
| stats earliest(soar_apply_time) as soar_t, latest(_time) as last_action by container_id, mac_address
| join mac_address [search index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=86001 anc_action=quarantine | rename _time as ise_apply_time]
| eval soar_to_ise_lag_s=ise_apply_time - soar_t
| stats avg(soar_to_ise_lag_s) as avg_lag, max(soar_to_ise_lag_s) as max_lag
```

## Visualization

MTTC trend; per-playbook success rate; failed-action log.

## Known False Positives

- SOAR action retries cause apparent duplicate apply.
- Endpoints already quarantined return success without a new 86001.

## References

- [Splunk SOAR Cisco ISE App](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4250)
